items.title: Two men held over KL shooting, firearm seized
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested two local men in Klang in connection with a shooting incident in the capital, seizing a firearm during the operation. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspects, aged 27 and 33, were detained at 11.45pm yesterday. Fadil said the first suspect has a record involving five prior cases, while the second has 17 prior cases.

items.title: Iran receives first revenue from Strait of Hormuz tolls
items.summary: TEHERAN: A senior Iranian parliament official said on Thursday that Tehran has received the first revenue from tolls it imposed on the strategic Strait of Hormuz in its war with the United States and Israel. "The first revenue received from the Strait of Hormuz tolls was deposited into the Central Bank account," said deputy speaker of parliament Hamidreza Hajibabaei, according to Tasnim news agency. Before the announcement of revenue from tolls, Iran's parliament had been deciding whether to impose them on shipping through the strait, with Iranian officials warning that maritime traffic through the strait would "not return to its pre-war status."

items.title: MACC seizes RM18.3mil in properties over NGO fund probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has seized immovable assets worth more than RM18.3 million to assist investigations into the alleged misappropriation of funds involving a non-governmental organisation (NGO). The commission, in a TikTok post, said the seizure involved 14 properties comprising seven residential units, three office spaces and four plots of land located at various locations. The total value of the assets seized is estimated at RM18.36 million based on current market valuations.

items.title: Level 1 hot weather alert issued for 10 areas in Perlis, Kedah and Kelantan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) today issued a Level 1 hot weather status or alert category for 10 areas involving Perlis, Kedah and Kelantan. MetMalaysia in a statement announced that the areas involved are the entire state of Perlis while in Kedah it involves the areas of Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Pendang and Sik. For Kelantan, the areas are Machang, Kuala Krai, Pasir Mas, Jeli and Tanah Merah.

items.title: Love, lies and laptops: Police bust love scam syndicate operating out of resort
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Police have busted a love scam syndicate targeting Singaporean men with the arrest of 58 people, all but two of whom are foreigners today. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the 53 men and five women were detained in a 4.30pm operation carried out by the state Commercial Crime Investigation Department. Yusoff said the syndicate, believed to have been operating for about a month, used women posing as Indonesians to lure victims.

items.title: Hormuz crisis throws spotlight on world's largest 'chokepoint': Strait of Malacca
items.summary: THE closure of the Strait of Hormuz has forced policymakers in Asia to face questions over the security of other maritime chokepoints, including the Strait of Malacca, which is the world's busiest waterway for international trade. WHAT IS THE MALACCA STRAIT? The 900-km (550-mile) long Malacca Strait, bounded by Indonesia, Thailand, Malaysia and Singapore, provides the shortest sea route from East Asia to the Middle East and Europe.

items.title: Johor eatery fined RM4k for defying health closure order
items.summary: KULAI: An eatery was fined RM4,000 by the magistrate's court here today after being found guilty of defying a closure order issued by health authorities last year. According to the charge, the person in charge of the café was accused of disobeying a closure order for unsanitary food at the premises along Jalan Kenanga 29/4, Taman Indahpura here, at 2.40pm on Feb 23, 2025. Meanwhile, the state health department said the case reflects the ongoing commitment of the Kulai district health office's inspectorate and legal unit to strengthen public health enforcement and ensure compliance among food operators.

items.title: Anwar receives courtesy call from Muslim scholars' delegation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today received a courtesy call from a delegation of the International Union of Muslim Scholars, led by its deputy president Muhammad Al-Hasan Al-Diddu Al-Shanqiti, at his office. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the visit is part of the scholars' programme in Malaysia, aimed at strengthening ties and exchanging views on pressing global issues. Anwar added that Muhammad Al-Hasan commended Malaysia's role in fostering unity beyond its borders.

items.title: Solarvest bags RM1.06bil solar project from Malakoff
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Solarvest Holdings Bhd has secured a RM1.06 billion contract to develop Malaysia's largest utility-scale solar project under the latest large-scale solar programme. Through its subsidiary Atlantic Blue Sdn Bhd, Solarvest has been appointed engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning (EPCC) partner and technology provider for the LSS5+ project awarded by Malakoff Corp Bhd via Malakoff Silver Solar Sdn Bhd. Solarvest said the project reflects the growing role of large-scale renewable infrastructure in stabilising the national grid while reducing reliance on conventional energy sources.

items.title: Police bust homestay hopping syndicate in Sarawak’s biggest drug haul to date
items.summary: KUCHING, April 23 — A drug syndicate’s tactic of renting homestay premises as packaging and storage centres has been uncovered after police arrested four individuals and seized various types of drugs worth about RM16.8 million in two separate operations around Kuching this month, making it the biggest seizure recorded in Sarawak to date. He said police also seized various types of drugs believed to be Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) weighing 107.5 kilogrammes (kg), ketamine (1.3kg), Erimin 5 pills (180 grammes), ecstasy pills (102 grammes) and syabu (28 grammes). Mohamad Zainal said the two suspects were believed to be involved in packaging and distributing drugs to locations designated by other syndicate members via WhatsApp.

items.title: Miri woman loses RM485,832 in Instagram scam
items.summary: MIRI, April 23 — A local woman lost RM485,832 after falling victim to a fraudulent online investment scheme that promised quick returns. Miri district police Chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the 48-year-old victim lodged a police report yesterday after realising she had been duped. He said the victim was initially drawn to an investment advertisement on Instagram before contacting an individual identified as “Han Zhi Hai” via an international phone number.

items.title: Armizan: 3 arrested, including 2 Indonesians, over alleged diesel misuse
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Enforcement authorities arrested a local man and two Indonesians for allegedly misusing fleet cards to stockpile diesel illegally at a premises here yesterday. Authorities seized approximately 11,500 litres of diesel worth RM58,880, along with an additional 1,200 litres found in an intermediate bulk container (IBC), valued at RM6,144. Armizan said other items seized included an IBC tank containing diesel, fuel pumps, hose equipment, a trailer tank, a four-wheel-drive vehicle, a bonded lorry and four IBC units loaded onto a lorry.

items.title: KPDN moves to fast-track prosecution of fuel smuggling offence
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) is streamlining its standard operating procedures (SOP) to expedite the prosecution of controlled goods misappropriation cases in court. Its minister, Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali, said the move follows a decision by the Enforcement Agencies Coordination Supreme Committee on April 6, which emphasised the need to accelerate legal action under the Control of Supplies Act 1961. Armizan said proposals include establishing fast-track trial channels, appointing dedicated judges, and coordinating case management between agencies to expedite case disposal.

items.title: Two former company directors charged over diesel investment scam, money laundering
items.summary: Shamsul Rijal is also facing a charge of receiving RM20,000 in proceeds from unlawful activities from the Spectrum Global bank account, cashed out through a bank account belonging to a 63-year-old woman. Before Judge Norma, Shamsul Rijal and Hamidah were jointly charged with receiving RM122,000 in proceeds from unlawful activities from the Spectrum Global bank account, which was cashed out through a bank account belonging to Reach Resource Management Plt. Hamidah faces an additional charge of receiving RM130,000 in proceeds from unlawful activities from the Spectrum Global bank account into her bank account, said to have occurred at a bank branch in KL City Centre between March 6, 2024, and March 29, 2024.

items.title: Duterte to face ICC trial after judges confirm charges
items.summary: THE HAGUE: Former Philippines president Rodrigo Duterte will face trial at the International Criminal Court after judges on Thursday confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his so-called "war on drugs." Pre-trial judges "unanimously confirmed all the charges... against Rodrigo Roa Duterte and committed him to trial," the ICC said in a statement. Duterte will be the first Asian former head of state to face trial at the ICC, which prosecutes individuals for the world's worst crimes such as war crimes and crimes against humanity.

items.title: Government freezes 163 unauthorised non-Muslim religious sites in KL, launches structured solution plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — The government has imposed an immediate freeze on any new construction or expansion at 163 unauthorised non-Muslim places of worship (RIBI) identified on public land in the city. Announcing the move, Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said 11 new sub-committees, led by local members of parliament, will be established to seek fair and structured solutions for these existing sites. The Federal Territories Department (JWP) has completed a mapping exercise, identifying 163 unauthorised RIBI across various parliamentary constituencies.

items.title: [UPDATED] Defence Minister says police to investigate soldier in fatal accident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has confirmed he is aware of a fatal crash on the Maju Expressway (MEX) involving an allegedly intoxicated soldier. However, he declined to comment further on the specifics of the incident, citing the ongoing police investigation. "Although it involves a soldier, we will let the police conduct the investigation.

items.title: Three-tier strategy for dignified, graceful ageing
items.summary: The National Health and Morbidity Survey 2025 paints a grim picture of the state of affairs of our elderly. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad has pointed out that Malaysia is ageing 1.5 times faster than Japan. Physical health is intrinsically linked to our environment.

items.title: Main suspect in Felda Kemahang shooting believed to have fled to Thailand
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: Police believe the main suspect involved in the shooting of a Felda settler's son on Monday has fled to Thailand. State police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the suspect, a Malaysian believed to be involved in cattle smuggling, crossed the border shortly after the incident in Felda Kemahang here. Yusoff said Kelantan police would seek assistance from their Thai counterparts to track down the suspect.

items.title: [UPDATED] Malaysia rejects external control of Strait of Malacca
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has reiterated that any decision concerning the Strait of Malacca must be made collectively by littoral states, dismissing suggestions of external control over the strategic waterway. "Any action involving the Strait of Malacca must be agreed upon by countries in this region… Indonesia, Singapore, Malaysia and Thailand," he told reporters after closing the Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security Asia (Natsec) 2026 exhibitions at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (Mitec) today. Earlier, Mohamad warned that any proposal to impose fees for passage through the Strait of Malacca could disrupt global trade and jeopardise international relations.

items.title: 17 injured in Danish train collision, five critical
items.summary: HILLERØD, Denmark: Two commuter trains collided head-on near the Danish capital on Thursday injuring 17, of whom five were in critical condition, emergency services said. "We can't provide any details for now about the cause of the accident," police official Morten Kaare Pedersen told reporters. He said police were alerted to the accident at 6.29am (0429 GMT), which occurred about 40 kilometres (25 miles) north of Copenhagen between the towns of Hillerod and Kagerup.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: IJM Corp eyes construction unit listing as early as next year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Corp Bhd may unlock value from its construction arm as early as next year, following the collapse of Sunway Bhd's takeover bid earlier this month. Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd (Kenanga IB) said the group is studying a possible listing of its construction business, with a final decision expected within the next two months. On other asset unlocking initiatives, Kenanga IB said IJM's strategic exit from India involves a two-year window to optimise disposal gains.

items.title: Malaysia has potential for sovereign rating update: S&P Global
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has the potential for a sovereign rating upgrade, supported by continuously narrowing fiscal deficits, S&P Global Ratings said. Its managing director and sector lead for sovereign ratings, Kim Eng Tan, however, said an upgrade is unlikely in the near term due to the ongoing global supply crisis, despite a stable economic outlook. He said this is due to Malaysia's external metrics, which measure foreign assets against foreign liabilities, appearing somewhat weaker than expected, given Malaysia's current account surplus.

items.title: Melaka police chief: Toddler tested positive after accidentally ingesting suspected drug-laced sweets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — A three-year-old girl is believed to have tested positive for drugs after consuming sweets suspected of containing narcotic substances, which were allegedly purchased by her social media influencer father. Melaka police chief Deputy Commissioner Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the 39-year-old suspect, who has nearly 600,000 online followers, has been arrested in connection with the case, The Star reported. The matter came to light on April 14 after a medical officer at Hospital Melaka treated the child, who was brought in in a semi-conscious state.

items.title: Sabah to take over environmental management powers under MA63 starting November, says Musa Aman
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 23 — Sabah is set to receive a transition of power regarding environmental management under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63), which is scheduled to be fully implemented in November, said Yang Dipertua Negeri Tun Musa Aman. Tun Musa said that in terms of carbon governance, the state government aims to be a leader in implementing Article 6 of the Paris Agreement in Malaysia by leveraging voluntary carbon market opportunities as a new source of funding to support forest and environmental conservation efforts. He said that in terms of land and property governance, the state government will table an amendment bill to the Land (Subsidiary Title) Enactment 1972 this year.

items.title: Gulf skirmish is squeezing the world's rubber
items.summary: The global rubber industry is about to be torn. At first glance, rubber seems far removed from the sandbanks of the Gulf. Rubber is a bulky, low-value-per-tonne commodity.

items.title: Mat Sabu: Malaysia-Vietnam ties key to stable food supply amid global shortages
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu says close Malaysia-Vietnam ties are vital to ensure the country's food supply remains stable amid global shortages triggered by the West Asia conflict. Mohamad expressed his commitment to bolstering global partnerships, broadening supply sources, and safeguarding the nation's food security. “Insya-Allah, I'll keep working to ensure our nation's food supply stays stable, sufficient, and secure for everyone's wellbeing,” he said.

items.title: DBKL proposes water detention measures after Bukit Lanjan landslide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has proposed an on-site water detention facility following the landslide in Kampung Bukit Lanjan. Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said the move aims to ensure surface runoff, particularly in the affected area, can be properly controlled. He said the proposal followed a site visit by his team.

items.title: Parents demand explanation over son's drowning
items.summary: Luqman Kamil Jamaluddin, 16, was found drowned on April 18 after being reported missing while searching for 'kepah' (mussels) with several hostel mates at the beach at 6pm on April 17. His father, Jamaluddin Wahudin, said many questions surrounding the incident remain unanswered, and the family has yet to receive any explanation from the school, the education department or the ministry. "My son lives in the school hostel, so who provided the boys with transport to the beach?

items.title: Three men arrested after police uncover 5,111 cartons of untaxed cigarettes in Seri Kembangan home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Police arrested three men and seized 5,111 cartons of various brands of cigarettes, believed to involve RM1.281 million in unpaid customs duties, in a raid under Op Taring Alpha 1 on a house in Taman Sri Serdang, Seri Kembangan, Selangor, yesterday. The Internal Security and Public Order Department’s Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Intelligence Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander, Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar, said the suspects, a supervisor and two workers, were aged between 34 and 43. Mohd Zaki said the cigarettes were found throughout the house, including the living room, bedrooms and storage areas.

items.title: Malaysia to target fuel smugglers with designated judges and fast-track trials
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 23 — The government is moving to expedite court proceedings for cases involving the smuggling of controlled goods, particularly petrol and diesel, to send a firm signal that it will not compromise on leakages that threaten national supply. Proposals have also been put forward for improvements at the judicial level, including creating a fast-track channel for smuggling cases, appointing designated judges to hear these cases, and coordinating case management teams between agencies. Armizan stressed that this aggressive action is a direct response to the global energy and supply crisis triggered by the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

items.title: L&G set for earnings rebound on strong development pipeline
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Land & General Bhd (L&G) is entering a phase of strong earnings recovery, supported by higher property progress billings and a solid development pipeline, according to TA Securities. Operationally, L&G's earnings momentum has strengthened significantly. Revenue grew 141.7 per cent to RM348.3 million, while margins improved to 16 per cent from 11 per cent.

items.title: Sainsbury's echoes Tesco warning as Iran war weighs on outlook
items.summary: LONDON: British supermarket group Sainsbury's warned on Thursday that uncertainty around how the Iran war will affect consumers is clouding its outlook and could drag profits lower this year, echoing concerns voiced by market leader Tesco. "The duration and extent of these impacts is very uncertain and this is reflected in our profit guidance," it said, forecasting total underlying operating profit of between 975 million pounds and 1.075 billion pounds (US$1.32-US$1.45 billion) for its 2026/27 year. Sainsbury's made retail underlying profit of 1.025 billion pounds in the year to February 28 2026, down 1.1 per cent on 2024/25 but in line with guidance.

items.title: Malaysia receives US$60.9mil as ADB channels US$9bil into Southeast Asia
items.summary: According to ADB's 2025 annual report, US$7.3 billion was channelled to public sector finance, while US$1.6 billion supported private sector initiatives. The Philippines emerged as the largest recipient in the region with US$6.81 billion, followed by Indonesia (US$4.39 billion), Vietnam (US$1.96 billion) and Thailand (US$1.45 billion). Malaysia received US$60.9 million.

items.title: Khazanah flags scams misusing its name, branding
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Khazanah Nasional Bhd has urged the public to remain vigilant against fraudulent investment schemes falsely claiming to be linked to the sovereign wealth fund. In a statement, Khazanah said scammers have been misusing its name, logo and branding across social media platforms and messaging applications to promote fake investment opportunities. Khazanah governance, risk and compliance head Datuk Suhana Dewi Selamat said the fund does not engage in any form of public fundraising or retail investment services.

items.title: E-hailing passenger fresh from KLIA killed in MEX wrong-way crash caused by drunk military officer
items.summary: The 31-year-old suspect exceeded the permitted alcohol limit in a breathalyser test conducted at the scene, according to Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa. The 20-year-old female passenger sustained bodily injuries and is currently receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL). The 41-year-old e-hailing driver also suffered head injuries.

items.title: Clearing Hormuz Strait mines could take six months: Report
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A Pentagon assessment said it could take six months to completely clear the Strait of Hormuz of Iranian-laid mines, which could keep oil prices high, the Washington Post reported on Wednesday. Even if hostilities end and the blockade lifts, it could take months to clear the waterway of mines, according to a Pentagon assessment, the Washington Post reported citing officials close to the discussion. Iran's parliament speaker said the Islamic republic would not reopen the strait as long as the US naval blockade remained.

items.title: MACC chief appointment must uphold integrity, public trust – MCW
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The appointment of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner is not merely about an individual but concerns the credibility of a key institution in the fight against corruption, abuse, and misconduct. Malaysian Corruption Watch (MCW) president, Jais Abdul Karim, stressed that any decision regarding the tenure or appointment of a new chief must be based on integrity, track record, and public confidence, not political pressure or perception. "Any decision to either not renew a contract or make a new appointment must be rooted in integrity, performance and public confidence, not mere perception," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Terengganu man admits to fatally injuring adopted son who died after caning
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 23 — A lorry attendant pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to two charges of causing injury and also for the death of his four-year-old adopted child. Muhammad Firdaus Zamri, 31, entered the plea before Judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli. On the first count, he was charged under Section 304(a) of the Penal Code for causing the death by negligence of Muhammad Adamdanial Abdul Khafidh at about 6 pm on April 11 this year at a house in Kampung Banggol Katong here.

items.title: First landslide in 30 years prompts DBKL to act on Bukit Lanjan flood risk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has proposed the creation of an on-site detention area as an immediate measure to address the risks of flooding and landslides in Kampung Bukit Lanjan here. Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said the proposal was among the main findings from a site visit conducted to formulate a mitigation plan for the area. Fadlun said DBKL’s main focus now is to control surface runoff, which is a contributing factor to slope instability, especially during heavy rain.

items.title: CAP urges urgent action over prolonged Sg Ketil pollution
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Consumers' Association of Penang (CAP) has expressed serious concern over the continued pollution of Sungai Ketil in Gua Musang, Kelantan, saying the problem has persisted for more than five years despite repeated complaints and regulatory attention. CAP said the ongoing contamination has caused significant ecological damage, with freshwater species such as kelah, baung and lampam among those most affected. CAP president Mohideen Abdul Kader said the association had repeatedly raised the issue with the Kelantan Department of Environment (DOE) and relevant local authorities, urging swift enforcement action.

items.title: Switzerland favours out-of-court settlement in Crans-Montana fire
items.summary: BERN: The Swiss government said on Wednesday that it favours an out-of-court settlement involving the New Year's fire disaster at a bar in the Crans-Montana ski resort that killed dozens of party-goers, many of them young people, reported German Press Agency (dpa). "Kurth has extensive experience steering highly complex processes involving many different stakeholders," the statement stated. A fire broke out during a New Year's Eve party in the Bar Le Constellation on New Year's night.

items.title: Another drink-driving case: Soldier causes crash, killing Bangladeshi, injuring three others [WATCH]
items.summary: A 22-year-old Bangladeshi man was killed, while four others, including the driver, were injured in the two-vehicle collision, said Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa. A 20-year-old female Bangladeshi passenger suffered injuries and is receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital. The e-hailing driver sustained head injuries, while a 36-year-old female passenger in the Ford Fiesta was also injured.

items.title: Exco: Selangor wants ‘win-win’ solutions for non-Muslim religious sites
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 23 — The Selangor government is adopting a diplomatic approach in addressing issues related to non-Muslim houses of worship (RIBI), aiming to ensure fair resolutions while preserving harmony among all parties. State Human Resources and Poverty Alleviation Committee chairman V. Papparaidu said authorities are currently handling 178 cases involving such facilities, all of which will be managed carefully with a win-win approach. “Selangor is a plural society.

items.title: Economy Ministry targets Malaysia’s supply chain stability to mitigate global conflict
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 23 — The government is focusing on safeguarding Malaysia’s supply chain to mitigate the impact of rising inflation, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said today. “So far, we have focused on managing the geopolitical impact in terms of the supply chain issues to ensure that the economic sector can operate unhindered,” he told reporters after officiating an Insolvency Department programme here today. Akmal explained that the core issue lies at the supply chain level and the availability of inputs for Malaysia’s product value chain.

items.title: No evidence of cancer cluster at UMMC, Health Ministry says after probe into viral claims
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 23 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has found no technical evidence to support allegations of a cancer cluster at the Universiti Malaya Medical Centre’s (UMMC) Nuclear Medicine Unit, following a thorough investigation sparked by a viral social media message. The ministry’s Medical Radiation Regulatory Division (MRRD) conducted the probe to assess radiation safety levels and compliance with the Atomic Energy Licensing Act 1984. The investigation concluded that radiation safety levels at the facility are safe, controlled, and present no critical compliance issues.

items.title: Economy minister: Over 31,500 bankruptcy cases since 2021, almost half due to personal loans
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said official data shows that 14,582 cases, or 46 per cent, were categorised as bankruptcies due to personal debt pressures. He said that 4,704 cases, or 15 per cent of the total, involved individuals aged 34 years and below. “The data highlights the importance of awareness about financial management and financial literacy from an early stage, especially among young families in planning their financial commitments based on their respective capabilities,” he said.

items.title: Mindef to build elite talent pool for asymmetric warfare, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) is intensifying its focus on developing the Cyber and Electromagnetic Command (PSEP) as part of a strategic shift towards modern warfare readiness, said its Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin today. Speaking at the National Defence University of Malaysia (UPNM) Forum held at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre (MITEC), Mohamed Khaled emphasised that a core pillar of this development is the cultivation of high-quality human capital through Professional Military Education. He said the establishment of PSEP is designed to align with the Malaysian Defence Intelligence Organisation (MDIO) to create a real-time, data-driven intelligence ecosystem.

items.title: Ayob Khan vows no cover-up over viral Kulim police arrest video
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 23 — The police will not cover up any misconduct involving its personnel, Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay pledged today, in response to a viral video showing an aggressive arrest in Kulim, Kedah. The deputy inspector-general of police confirmed that two separate investigations, one criminal and one internal, are already underway regarding the incident, which depicted a police officer pulling a man off a motorcycle and stepping on his head. During the arrest, police found two plastic packets believed to contain ketum in the suspect’s motorcycle basket.

items.title: Former RTM radio presenter Mariamah Tijo dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Former Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM) Radio presenter Mariamah Tijo died early this morning. “My mother had several medical conditions, including a stroke four years ago, but was admitted to the hospital two days ago due to breathing difficulties,” he said when contacted by Bernama today. — Bernama

items.title: Not a producer, but a pit stop: DIGP says Malaysia still major drug transit point
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 23 — Malaysia remains a major transit point for international drug trafficking syndicates, as evidenced by narcotics seizures worth RM3.1 billion in 2025 alone, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said today. Ayob Khan identified northern states like Penang and Kedah as primary transit locations due to their proximity to the international border. The deputy IGP further highlighted a shift in strategy, with police now using laws designed to combat organised crime to dismantle entire drug networks.

items.title: No, Khazanah is not hitting you up on Telegram with a hot investment tip
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Khazanah Nasional Berhad has issued a public warning about scammers misusing the sovereign wealth fund’s name, logo, and identities of its senior leadership to lure victims into fraudulent investment schemes. These scams often promote fake investment opportunities, promising quick and unrealistic returns. “Khazanah does not offer public investment schemes or solicit funds from individuals.

items.title: Clean house or lose trust: Deputy IGP says even minor police misconduct can tarnish entire force’s reputation
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 23 — Disciplinary action was taken against 1,429 police officers and personnel between January and December last year, said Deputy Inspector-General of Police Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay. Earlier, in his speech, he said firm enforcement against misconduct was necessary to preserve public confidence in the police force. This includes national leadership,” he said.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan assembly sitting adjourned after royal address
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun today said the First Sitting of the 15th Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly will be adjourned to a later date The sitting, which was officiated by Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir this morning, was originally scheduled to run from April 27 to 29. “The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly sitting has been adjourned to a suitable date depending on the situation,” he briefly told reporters after the opening ceremony. Meanwhile, State Assembly Speaker Datuk MK Ibrahim Abd Rahman said today’s ceremony solely involved His Royal Highness’s royal address.

items.title: Keep calm and follow the law, Tuanku Muhriz says amid Negeri Sembilan tensions
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today called for calm, restraint and strict adherence to the law amid ongoing developments involving Negri Sembilan’s institutional and administrative landscape. Delivering his royal address at the opening of the First Sitting of the 15th Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly at Wisma Negeri here, Tuanku Muhriz stressed that all actions and decisions, particularly by those in positions of authority, must be grounded in facts, proper procedures and established legal frameworks. A general view of the opening of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly in Seremban on April 23, 2026.

items.title: King says he will determine who is next MACC chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia today said he will determine the best candidate to become the next chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). In a Facebook post today, he said that the MACC chief's post was important to ensure the body continues to function effectively as the country’s main institution in fighting corruption, misappropriation and abuse of power. The current MACC chief, Tan Sri Azam Baki, is due to retire when his current one-year contract ends on May 12.

items.title: Moving on: Sabah DCM Masidi says GRS unfazed by Usno exit
items.summary: The response comes after Pandikar announced that his United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) was leaving GRS, citing differences in direction and policy alignment following the recent state election. Sabah Deputy Chief Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun, who is also the GRS secretary-general, said the coalition is moving ahead regardless of Usno’s decision. With Usno’s departure, GRS now comprises five component parties: Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Cinta Sabah, and Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah.

items.title: Goldman Sachs ends 1MDB fraud lawsuit with agreement-in-principle
items.summary: Goldman declined to comment. US and Malaysian ⁠authorities have said US$4.5 billion (RM18 billion) was siphoned ⁠away from ⁠1MDB, with some diverted to offshore bank accounts and shell companies linked to Low. Goldman helped 1MDB sell US$6.5 billion of bonds, ⁠and collected an estimated US$600 million of fees.

items.title: Four Undangs skip Negeri Sembilan assembly opening as royal row lingers
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — The four Undangs of Negeri Sembilan were not in attendance at the opening of the State Legislative Assembly today, a departure from custom amid ongoing issues within the state’s royal institution. Despite their absence, the ceremony, officiated by Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir at the Wisma Negeri state secretariat, proceeded as scheduled. The state government, however, has maintained that the administration of Negeri Sembilan remains unchanged, with Tuanku Muhriz continuing to carry out his official duties.

items.title: Maritime corridor for Gaza: Malaysia joins global push to break unlawful blockade
items.summary: BRUSSELS, April 23 — A Malaysian delegation joined international parliamentarians and stakeholders in endorsing the Brussels Declaration at the Global Sumud Parliamentary Congress, signalling a collective push for stronger adherence to international law on Palestine and Lebanon. The declaration affirms that international law must be applied consistently and in good faith, stressing that responsibility extends beyond recognition to concrete action in protecting affected populations and restoring dignity and self-determination. The declaration also reiterates the inalienable rights of Palestinians and the Lebanese people, including sovereignty, accountability, reparations and control over natural resources.
